cot_prefix: Okay, so Jane quit her job on March 20, 2020. We need to find out what the date is 176 days later, and then add one more day to get tomorrow's date. Let me break this down step by step. First, starting from March 20, 2020. Let's count the days in each month until we reach 176 days. March has 31 days, so from March 20 to March 31 is 11 days (since March 20 is day 0, March 21 is day 1, ..., March 31 is day 11). Wait, no. Wait, if Jane quit on March 20, that's day 0. Then day 1 is March 21, and so on. So from March 20 to March 31 is 12 days (including March 21 up to March 31). Wait, let me check again. March 20 is day 0. Then March 21 is day 1, March 22 day 2, ..., March 31 is day 12. So 12 days in March. Then, April has 30 days. So adding those 30 days to the 12 days in March gives 42 days so far (12 + 30 = 42). Then May has 31 days. Adding that gives 42 + 31 = 73 days. June has 30 days, so 73 + 30 = 103 days. July has 31 days, so 103 + 31 = 134 days. August has 31 days, so 134 + 31 = 165 days. Then September has 30 days. We need 176 days total. So we've already accounted for 165 days by the end of August. So 176 - 165 = 11 days in September. So the 176th day is September 11, 2020. Wait, no. Wait, if we add 31 days for August to the 134 days (from March 20 to July 31), that brings us to August 31, which is 165 days. Then the next day is September 1. So from September 1 to September X is 166 days. So we need 176 days total. So September has 30 days. 176 - 165 = 11. So September 1 is day 166, day 167 is September 2,... day 165 + 11 = 176, which would be September 11, 2020. Therefore, 176 days after March 20, 2020 is September 11, 2020. But we need tomorrow's date. Wait, no. Wait, the problem states that 176 days have passed since March 20, 2020, and asks for the date tomorrow. So if 176 days have passed since March 20, that is the current date. Then tomorrow would be 177 days after March 20. So let me rephrase. The current date is 176 days after March 20, 2020. Tomorrow is one day after that, so 177 days after March 20. Let me calculate that. Alternatively, maybe I made an error in counting above. Let me check using another method. Using a date calculator might help, but since I can't use external tools, I'll proceed manually. March 20, 2020 is a Friday. Let me count the days: March: 31 - 20 = 11 days remaining in March. Wait, no. Wait, from March 20 to March 31 is 12 days (including March 20). But if March 20 is day 0, then March 31 is day 11. Because March 21 is day 1, March 22 day 2, ..., March 31 is day 12. Wait, I'm getting confused. Let me think again. If today is March 20, then 1 day after is March 21. So the number of days from March 20 to March 20 is 0 days. From March 20 to March 21 is 1 day. From March 20 to March 31 is 12 days (since there are 12 days from March 21 to March 31). So 12 days in March. April has 30 days. So 12 + 30 = 42 days by April 30. May has 31 days. 42 + 31 = 73 days by May 31. June has 30 days. 73 + 30 = 103 days by June 30. July has 31 days. 103 + 31 = 134 days by July 31. August has 31 days. 134 + 31 = 165 days by August 31.
